label: SWOT Analysis
description: Produce a rigorous, evidence-based SWOT with strategic implications

fields.label: Subject of analysis
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics' position in mid-market BI

fields.label: Business context
fields.placeholder: Series A SaaS, $14M ARR, strong product, weak brand, well-funded incumbents.

fields.label: Strategic objective
fields.placeholder: Decide whether to expand into enterprise next year

fields.label: Key competitors
fields.placeholder: Looker, Metabase, Tableau

template:
You are a strategy consultant. Produce a rigorous SWOT analysis of {{subject}}.

## Context
{{context}}
{{#if objective}}- Strategic objective framing the analysis: {{objective}}.
{{/if}}{{#if competitors}}- Key competitors to benchmark against: {{competitors}}.
{{/if}}{{#if lens}}- Analyze through these lenses: {{lens}}.
{{/if}}
## Standards
- Strengths and weaknesses are internal and relative to competitors; opportunities and threats are external.
- Every point is specific and evidence-backed (a fact, metric, or trend), not a platitude.
- Rank items within each quadrant by strategic significance.
{{#if tows}}- Add a TOWS matrix translating the SWOT into concrete strategies (SO, ST, WO, WT).
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The four quadrants, ranked, with evidence for each item.
{{#if tows}}2. A TOWS action set with the 3-5 highest-leverage moves.
{{else}}2. The 3-5 highest-leverage strategic implications.
{{/if}}3. The single most important strategic question this analysis raises.

Be decisive and specific; cut generic points. Where evidence is thin, label the item as an assumption rather than asserting it.

label: Market Analysis
description: Size a market and assess demand, competition and entry attractiveness

fields.label: Market / segment
fields.placeholder: Embedded analytics for vertical SaaS

fields.label: Why you're analyzing this
fields.placeholder: Evaluating whether to launch an embeddable analytics SDK as a new line.

fields.label: Geography
fields.placeholder: North America + Western Europe

fields.label: Known data points
fields.placeholder: ~12k vertical SaaS firms in scope; analytics budget ~3-5% of R&D.

template:
You are a market research analyst. Analyze the market for {{market}}{{#if geography}} in {{geography}}{{/if}}, for a {{audience}} audience.

## Purpose
{{context}}
{{#if knownData}}
## Known data points
{{knownData}}
{{/if}}
## Scope
{{#if components}}- Cover: {{components}}.
{{/if}}- For sizing, build TAM/SAM/SOM bottom-up with stated assumptions and a sanity-check from the top down.
- For competition, map the major players, their positioning, and where the gaps are.
- Distinguish facts (cite or label the basis) from estimates and assumptions.

## Deliverables
1. An executive summary with the 3-5 findings that matter most.
2. The full analysis by component, quantified where possible, with an assumptions register.
{{#if recommendation}}3. A clear entry recommendation (enter / wait / avoid) with the rationale and the key risks.
{{else}}3. The strategic implications and the most important open questions.
{{/if}}
Be quantitative and explicit about assumptions. Proceed with reasoned estimates rather than refusing for lack of data; clearly flag low-confidence figures.

label: Business Case
description: Build a decision-ready business case with options, costs and ROI

fields.label: Initiative / investment
fields.placeholder: Build an in-house customer data platform

fields.label: Problem & desired outcome
fields.placeholder: Fragmented customer data slows go-to-market; want a single source of truth in 9 months.

fields.label: Options to evaluate
fields.placeholder: 1) Build in-house 2) Buy Segment 3) Do nothing / status quo.

fields.label: Known costs & resources
fields.placeholder: Build: 4 engineers x 9 months + ~$30k infra/yr. Buy: ~$120k/yr.

fields.label: Expected benefits
fields.placeholder: ~15% faster campaign launch; reduced data-eng toil; better targeting.

fields.label: Evaluation horizon
fields.placeholder: 3 years

label: Strategy Deck
description: Outline a tight, narrative-driven strategy presentation slide by slide

fields.label: Deck topic
fields.placeholder: FY2027 growth strategy

fields.label: Core thesis / recommendation
fields.placeholder: Concentrate spend on mid-market upmarket motion; sunset the self-serve free tier.

fields.label: Supporting context & data
fields.placeholder: Mid-market NRR 121% vs self-serve 78%; CAC payback 14 vs 31 months.

template:
You are a management consultant who builds executive-grade strategy decks. Outline a deck on "{{topic}}" for a {{audience}} audience.

## Core thesis
{{thesis}}
{{#if context}}
## Supporting context
{{context}}
{{/if}}
## Deck design
- Length: {{length}}. Narrative structure: {{framework}}.
- Lead with the answer; every slide must advance the argument, not just present data.
- For each slide give: an action-title (the takeaway as a full sentence), the key content/visual, and the supporting evidence.
{{#if speakerNotes}}- Include concise speaker notes per slide (what to say, what to anticipate).
{{/if}}- End with a clear ask and next steps.

## Deliverables
1. The slide-by-slide outline with action-titles and content.
2. The one-sentence storyline that connects every action-title in order.
3. A note of the 2-3 charts or proof points that most need real data.

Make the argument tight and decisive. Proceed with reasoned placeholders where data is missing and label them clearly.

label: Executive Memo
description: Write a crisp, recommendation-first memo for senior decision-makers

fields.label: Memo subject
fields.placeholder: Recommendation: pause the EU expansion until H2

fields.label: Your recommendation
fields.placeholder: Delay EU launch by two quarters and redirect spend to mid-market US.

fields.label: Reasoning & evidence
fields.placeholder: EU pipeline 60% below plan; localization unfinished; US payback far better.

fields.label: Decision or action requested
fields.placeholder: Approve the reprioritization at Thursday's leadership review

template:
You are a chief of staff writing an executive memo for a {{audience}} reader, in a {{tone}} register.

## Subject
{{subject}}

## Recommendation
{{recommendation}}

## Reasoning
{{rationale}}

## Memo standards
- Length: {{length}}. Recommendation and the ask appear in the first three sentences.
- BLUF (bottom line up front), then the supporting argument in ranked order of importance.
{{#if alternatives}}- Briefly state the alternatives considered and why they were rejected.
{{/if}}{{#if decision}}- Make the requested decision explicit: {{decision}}. Include what happens if it is deferred.
{{/if}}- Tight prose, no filler, no hedging; quantify claims where possible.

## Deliverables
1. The complete memo, ready to send.
2. A one-line subject and a single-sentence summary suitable for a calendar invite.

Be direct and decisive. Use the evidence given; flag any critical unknown as an explicit risk rather than padding the memo.

label: OKRs
description: Draft sharp, measurable objectives and key results for a team or company

fields.label: Team / owner name
fields.placeholder: Growth Marketing team

fields.label: Strategic context & priorities
fields.placeholder: Company goal: reach $20M ARR. Our role: improve pipeline volume and quality.

fields.label: Current baselines & metrics
fields.placeholder: MQLs 800/qtr, SQL conversion 18%, pipeline $4.5M.

template:
You are an operating-cadence coach. Draft {{period}} OKRs for {{owner}} at the {{scope}} level.

## Strategic context
{{context}}
{{#if baseline}}
## Baselines
{{baseline}}
{{/if}}
## OKR standards
- Produce {{count}} objectives. Objectives are qualitative, inspiring, and aligned to the strategic context.
- Each objective has 2-4 key results that are measurable, outcome-based (not task lists), with a from -> to target.
- Ambition: {{ambition}}. State the success threshold for each key result.
{{#if initiatives}}- For each objective, list the 2-3 key initiatives expected to move the results.
{{/if}}- Reject vanity metrics and unmeasurable language; every KR must be objectively scorable.

## Deliverables
1. The full OKR set, formatted clearly by objective.
2. A one-line note on how this set ladders up to the company strategy.
3. Any KR where the baseline is missing, flagged with a sensible proposed target.

Be specific and outcome-focused. Propose concrete numeric targets using the baselines; where a baseline is unknown, state the assumption rather than leaving it vague.

label: Go-to-Market Plan
description: Build a focused GTM plan covering segment, motion, channels and metrics

fields.label: Product / offering
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics Embedded SDK

fields.label: What it is & why now
fields.placeholder: An embeddable analytics SDK for vertical SaaS; launching to expand TAM.

fields.label: Target customer / ICP
fields.placeholder: Series B+ vertical SaaS, 50-500 employees, has a product/eng team.

fields.label: Pricing & packaging direction
fields.placeholder: Usage-based, free dev tier, enterprise contract above 1M events/mo

fields.label: Launch timeline
fields.placeholder: Beta in 6 weeks, GA in Q3

template:
You are a go-to-market strategist. Build a GTM plan for {{offering}} with a primarily {{motion}} motion.

## What it is & why now
{{context}}

## Ideal customer profile
{{icp}}

## Plan components
- Positioning and a one-sentence value proposition for the ICP, plus the core differentiation.
- Segmentation and prioritization: which segment to win first and why.
{{#if channels}}- Channel strategy across: {{channels}}. For each, the role, expected motion, and a first 90-day play.
{{/if}}{{#if pricing}}- Pricing & packaging direction: {{pricing}}.
{{/if}}{{#if timeline}}- Phased rollout aligned to: {{timeline}}.
{{/if}}{{#if metrics}}- Success metrics with explicit targets and the leading indicators to watch.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The GTM plan organized by component.
2. A 90-day launch roadmap with owners and milestones.
3. The top 3 risks and the assumptions to validate before scaling spend.

Be specific and prioritized; avoid doing everything at once. Proceed with reasoned defaults and label assumptions; ask only if a decision is genuinely blocking.

label: Porter's Five Forces
description: Industry attractiveness analysis using Porter's Five Forces.

fields.label: Company / business unit
fields.placeholder: Northwind (vertical SaaS for clinics)

fields.label: Industry & scope
fields.placeholder: Practice-management software for small healthcare clinics in North America.

fields.label: Market context & dynamics
fields.placeholder: Fragmented buyers, 2 large incumbents, rising switching costs, AI entrants emerging.

fields.label: Known facts per force (optional)
fields.placeholder: Suppliers: few cloud vendors; Buyers: price-sensitive solo clinics.

template:
You are a strategy consultant applying Porter's Five Forces rigorously for {{company}}.

## Industry
{{industry}}

## Market context
{{context}}

{{#if knownForces}}## Known inputs per force
{{knownForces}}

{{/if}}## Analysis requirements
- Audience: {{audience}}. Depth: {{depth}}.
- Analyze all five forces: competitive rivalry, threat of new entrants, threat of substitutes, supplier power, buyer power.
- For each force, give the key drivers and the evidence/reasoning, not just a verdict.
{{#if scoreForces}}- Rate each force High / Medium / Low with a one-line justification.
{{/if}}- Synthesize into an overall view of industry attractiveness.
{{#if implications}}- Translate the analysis into 3-4 concrete strategic implications and where to compete.
{{/if}}- Flag the biggest assumption that, if wrong, flips the conclusion.

## Deliverables
1. The five-forces analysis, force by force.
2. An overall attractiveness verdict with rationale.
3. Strategic implications and the key open question.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Vision & Mission Statement
description: Crisp vision, mission and guiding principles for a company or team.

fields.label: Company / team name
fields.placeholder: Northstar

fields.label: What you do & for whom
fields.placeholder: We give small clinics enterprise-grade software so they can focus on patients.

fields.label: Change you want to see in the world
fields.placeholder: Healthcare admin is invisible; clinicians spend time with patients, not paperwork.

fields.label: Core values / beliefs
fields.placeholder: Patients first, radical simplicity, earn trust daily.

template:
You are a brand and strategy advisor who crafts language leaders rally behind. Create vision and mission statements for {{orgName}} at the {{scope}} level.

## What we do
{{whatWeDo}}

## The future we want
{{future}}

{{#if values}}## Values input
{{values}}

{{/if}}## Crafting rules
- Tone: {{tone}}. Vision horizon: {{horizon}}.
- Vision = the future-state outcome (aspirational, not about the product). Mission = how we get there (concrete, present tense).
- Make them memorable and specific; ruthlessly cut jargon, buzzwords, and hedging.
- Each statement should be one or two sentences and pass a "would a team actually say this" test.
{{#if includePrinciples}}- Add 4-6 guiding principles, each a short imperative with a one-line meaning.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. Final vision statement (plus 2 alternates).
2. Final mission statement (plus 2 alternates).
3. {{#if includePrinciples}}The guiding principles list.{{else}}A one-line internal rallying cry.{{/if}}

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Strategic Partnership Proposal
description: Proposal pitching a mutually beneficial partnership to a target company.

fields.label: Your company
fields.placeholder: Northstar Insights

fields.label: Target partner
fields.placeholder: Brightline CRM

fields.label: Why this partnership, why now
fields.placeholder: Shared mid-market customers; our analytics fills a gap in their roadmap.

fields.label: Value to the partner
fields.placeholder: New revenue line, stickier product, differentiated analytics for their buyers.

fields.label: Value to us
fields.placeholder: Distribution to 12k their customers, co-sell motion, category credibility.

fields.label: Proposed commercial model
fields.placeholder: Rev share 80/20; co-marketing fund; mutual SLAs

template:
You are a corporate development lead pitching a partnership. Write a partnership proposal from {{ourCompany}} to {{partner}} for a {{partnerType}} partnership, for an audience of {{audience}}.

## Why now
{{rationale}}

## Value exchange
- For {{partner}}: {{valueToPartner}}
- For {{ourCompany}}: {{valueToUs}}

## Proposal rules
- Lead with the partner's upside before ours; make the win-win concrete and quantified where possible.
- Cover: strategic fit, the joint offering, target customers, go-to-market motion, and success metrics.
{{#if model}}- Propose a commercial model: {{model}} (frame as a starting point, not an ultimatum).
{{else}}- Suggest a sensible commercial model as a starting point.
{{/if}}- Include a phased plan (pilot -> scale) with clear next steps and owners.
- Be specific and credible; avoid hype and vague synergy language.

## Deliverables
1. The partnership proposal.
2. A 5-bullet executive summary to open with.
3. The recommended first ask and pilot scope.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.
